This list, crucial for those under adjudication, will be reviewed by the Supreme Court on Tuesday. The process involved 705 judges scrutinizing applications, with approved names marked for addition and rejected ones for deletion.

The film's gripping narrative and strong performances are driving its phenomenal success.

title: No suitable Indian option? Why KKR are yet to replace injured Harshit Rana
description: Earlier, KKR had also roped in Zimbabwe pacer Blessing Muzarabani in place of Bangladesh pacer Mustafizur Rahman. KKR had initially signed Mustafizur for Rs 9.2 crore as a backup overseas pacer to Rs 18 crore recruit Matheesha Pathirana, but the Bangladesh left-armer was released following BCCI directives earlier this year amid heightened India-Bangladesh tensions.

title: CBI, ED showing reluctance in ADAG probe: SC
description: The Supreme Court has expressed strong disapproval of probe agencies' perceived hesitation in investigating Anil Ambani's ADAG group for alleged Rs 40,000 crore loan fraud. Directing CBI and ED to expedite a coordinated inquiry, the court emphasized transparency and fairness. Fresh status reports are mandated within four weeks, with the court urging action against all involved in irregular loan sanctions.

title: SC: Mamata government hell-bent on impeding Kolkata Metro project
description: The Supreme Court sternly criticized the West Bengal government for obstructing the Kolkata Metro Orange Line's completion, calling their appeal "frivolous" and an "obstinate anti-development agenda." The court rejected the state's plea for time until May, citing elections, and emphasized the government's dereliction of duty in delaying a vital public transport project.

title: Parliament seats may be increased to 816 for 1/3rd women's reservation
description: The government plans to introduce bills for one-third women's reservation in Lok Sabha and assembly polls, potentially increasing Lok Sabha seats to 816. This move aims to empower women and reshape the political landscape. Implementation hinges on delimitation, with a proposal to use the 2011 census data to ensure the quota is effective by the 2029 elections.

title: Conflict posing unprecedented challenges for India, PM Modi tells Parliament
description: Prime Minister Modi highlighted India's stance on the West Asia conflict in Parliament. He stressed opposition to attacks on infrastructure and trade routes. India advocates for diplomacy and peaceful resolution. Efforts are underway to ensure safe passage for Indian ships and secure vital supplies like oil and gas. The government is in contact with international partners to maintain maritime safety.

title: 2 India-flagged tankers clear Hormuz; Iran denies taking ransom
description: Two Indian-flagged LPG tankers, Jag Vasant and Pine Gas, carrying substantial cooking gas, successfully navigated the war-torn Strait of Hormuz. This marks the fourth Indian vessel passage since the conflict's onset, with four more Indian ships and crews awaiting safe transit.  The government is prioritizing their secure passage amid regional tensions.

title: PM Modi: India has strategic petro reserves of over 53 lakh tonne
description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi assured that the government is actively working to maintain uninterrupted supplies of essential commodities like petrol, diesel, gas, and fertilizers amidst West Asian conflicts. Diversified energy import sources, increased ethanol blending, and extensive railway electrification have significantly reduced dependence on fossil fuels, bolstering India's energy security.

title: Your next LPG refill could be of 10kg instead of regular 14.2kg
description: Oil marketing companies are reportedly considering delivering underweight LPG cylinders to ensure more equitable distribution amid critical availability. While the petroleum ministry dismisses these reports as speculative, refiners confirm the proposal is under government consideration. Increased domestic production is now meeting a higher percentage of demand, despite disruptions in West Asia impacting imports.

title: Fuel frenzy in Gujarat: Thousands rush to petrol pumps across cities amid shortage rumours
description: Panic buying gripped Gujarat as rumours of a fuel shortage led to long queues and temporary rationing at petrol pumps. Dealers cited operational shifts, including advance payment requirements and weekend replenishment gaps, as reasons for temporary disruptions. Officials and oil companies have reassured the public that sufficient fuel stocks are available across the state, urging citizens to avoid unnecessary panic.

This unexpected absence impacts the surging Lakers, who are on a nine-game win streak.  The Pistons, leading the East, will also be without star Cade Cunningham, creating a pivotal matchup.

title: Nirav Modi appears in person to fight Rs 100 crore Bank of India case in London
description: Fugitive jeweller Nirav Modi appeared in a London court to contest a Rs 100 crore claim by Bank of India. The bank seeks repayment of a loan to his company, Firestar Diamond, for which Modi provided a personal guarantee.

Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Fact check: Did a former employee expose Candace Owens behavior?
description: A former employee has accused Candace Owens of “narcissistic” behavior, but the claims remain unverified. No evidence, official response, or independent confirmation has been provided. The allegations mention Turning Point USA and Blexit, but nothing has been proven. Social media remains divided, and the situation is still unclear with no investigation or legal action so far.

title: Elliotte Friedman hints at James Hagens’ signing decision coming soon
description: NHL Insider Elliotte Friedman hinted at a decision regarding top prospect James Hagens' professional future by Monday. While Hagens, the Bruins' seventh overall pick in 2025, has impressed, the team might hesitate to rush him into the NHL roster during their playoff push. Friedman believes Hagens is a great fit, but the exact path, NHL or AHL, remains uncertain.

title: 'Torso found near canal': Delhi woman beheaded in Bulandshahr; lover among 2 arrested
description: Two men were arrested for allegedly beheading a Delhi woman and dumping her body in Bulandshahr. The prime accused, her live-in partner Vikas Kumar, confessed to the crime, stating he was pressured by the victim to stay with her. He, along with accomplices, decapitated her to conceal her identity.

title: Who is AmineMaTue? French streamer Mohamed Amine Mahmoud joins Sidemen Charity Match
description: AmineMaTue, whose real name is Mohamed Amine Mahmoud, will play in the Sidemen Charity Match 2026 at Wembley Stadium. The French streamer is known for his Twitch content and football events. He joins Sidemen FC after being picked in the draft. His entry is expected to bring more viewers, though some fans have mixed reactions.

Gandhi also voiced concerns over a trade deal he claims will harm Indian farmers and alleged the BJP-RSS are undermining tribal rights by promoting the term 'Vanvasi'.

Despite Brown’s on-gridiron frustrations, analysts suggest he could be the WR1 Cleveland desperately needs, potentially costing a high draft pick. The Browns are known for acquiring problematic players like Deshaun Watson. They may face significant financial considerations for the veteran receiver. The Patriots who were also eyeing Brown, are out of competition now.

The exact number of victims and the cause of the crash are still under investigation.

Trump announced a five-day pause on strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure, citing ongoing discussions, while military actions continued on the ground.

title: H-1B registration dips 30-50%: Companies that are emerging as ‘favourites’ in new system
description: H-1B visa registrations for FY27 saw a 30-50% drop, reflecting employer caution due to higher fees and a new wage-based lottery. This shift benefits mid-tier IT firms and U.S. Master's degree holders, leveling the playing field against large outsourcing giants and prioritizing specialized talent.

title: First human patient with Elon Musk's Neuralink brain chip shares 100 days update
description: Noland Arbaugh, the first recipient of Neuralink's brain implant, celebrated 100 days of using the device.  Paralyzed since 2016, he now controls a MacBook and plays World of Warcraft solely with his thoughts. Arbaugh described the experience as "science fiction that somehow became my everyday reality," highlighting newfound independence and a transformed life.
